100089226.description: Filters allow data objects to specify test criteria which allows their use. Filters can be defined by a single object of type (Filter Test), an array of tests, collection groups, or a combination of these objects.

AND.description: Evaluates all tests in the group, all must pass in order for the group to pass.

NOT.description: Evaluates all tests in the group, all must fail in order for the group to pass.

OR.description: Evaluates tests in the group, one or more must pass in order for the group to pass.

all.description: Evaluates all tests in the group, all must pass in order for the group to pass.

all_of.description: Evaluates all tests in the group, all must pass in order for the group to pass.

any.description: Evaluates tests in the group, one or more must pass in order for the group to pass.

any_of.description: Evaluates tests in the group, one or more must pass in order for the group to pass.

none_of.description: Evaluates all tests in the group, all must fail in order for the group to pass.

100089226.title: Filter Group Map

domain.description: The domain the test should be performed in.

subject.description: The subject of this filter test.

test.description: The name of the test to apply.

value.description: The value being compared with the test.

filters.description: Conditions that must be met for this preset to be applied.

kick_distance_threshold.description: Maximum horizontal distance at which another entity can kick this entity. Only used when push_mode is "ball".

kick_speed_scale.description: Multiplier applied to the pushing entity's movement speed to determine kick force. Only used when push_mode is "ball".

max_kick_speed.description: Maximum speed the ball can be kicked at, regardless of how fast the pushing entity is moving. Only used when push_mode is "ball".

min_distance.description: Minimum distance between two entities for push forces to be applied. Entities closer than this will not push each other.

min_kick_speed.description: Minimum speed the ball will be kicked at, regardless of how slowly the pushing entity is moving. Only used when push_mode is "ball".

play_sound_cooldown_in_seconds.description: Cooldown in seconds between sounds. A lower number results in more sounds.

play_sound_impulse_threshold.description: Minimum change of velocity needed to trigger the push sound. A lower value means higher sensitivity.

push_mode.description:
Defines the type of push vector calculation applied to the entity:
- "default": Standard push calculation used by most entities.
- "legacy_boat": Legacy push calculation historically used by boats. Includes dampened forces and sneak-based cancellation.
- "legacy_minecart": Legacy push calculation historically used by minecarts. Includes alignment-based collision handling and velocity averaging.- "ball": Push calculation for ball-like entities. The ball is kicked in the direction of the pushing entity's movement, with force based on their movement speed.
push_mode.title: PushMode

push_scale_other.description: Scales how much push force this entity applies to the other entity when colliding. A value of 1.0 applies full force, 0.5 applies half.

push_scale_self.description: Scales how much this entity pushes itself away when colliding with another entity. A value of 1.0 applies full force, 0.5 applies half.

strength_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to the push strength. Higher values result in stronger pushes.

vertical_kick_multiplier.description: Multiplier for the upward force applied when the ball is kicked while on the ground. A value of 0 keeps the ball flat. Only used when push_mode is "ball".

2789112004.description: Filters allow data objects to specify test criteria which allows their use. Filters can be defined by a single object of type (Filter Test), an array of tests, collection groups, or a combination of these objects.

items.description: Filters allow data objects to specify test criteria which allows their use. Filters can be defined by a single object of type (Filter Test), an array of tests, collection groups, or a combination of these objects.

oneOf.description: Filters allow data objects to specify test criteria which allows their use. Filters can be defined by a single object of type (Filter Test), an array of tests, collection groups, or a combination of these objects.

2789112004.title: Filter Group

description: Allows an entity to be pushed by other entities.

presets.description: Defines how this entity behaves when pushed by another entity. The first preset whose "filter" conditions are met will be applied; if none match, a default configuration is used instead.

title: minecraft:pushable_by_entity
